_comment: LeRoy autonomy defaults. Philosophy: ship the working car; the user turns on autonomy and picks options. Non-burning, safe features are ON by default. Token-burning / autonomous features are OFF by default and offered a la carte during `leroy init`. NOTHING autonomous is installed or scheduled until the user approves it. The wizard writes the user's actual picks to ~/.claude/config/autonomy.json; only enabled features get wired later (via `leroy enable <feature>`).

on_by_default._comment: Good, non-burning features. Installed and active out of the box. No timers, no autonomous token spend.

self_growing_memory.label: Self-growing memory
self_growing_memory.plain: I remember what we work on and get sharper every session — no save button, ever.

self_heal_observe.label: Self-heal (observe only)
self_heal_observe.plain: I watch my own health and note problems, but I do not fix anything on a timer without you.
self_heal_observe.token_cost: none (observation only; the timed auto-fix loop is the OFF-by-default 'scheduled_automations' item)

gate.label: Session gate
gate.plain: Every request routes through one front door so nothing gets skipped.

routing.label: Smart routing
routing.plain: I send each task to the right specialist automatically.

mcp_builder.label: MCP builder
mcp_builder.plain: You can say 'talk to my Notion' and I will help wire the connector.

off_by_default._comment: Autonomous / token-burning features. Offered a la carte during onboarding, each DEFAULT NO. Recorded but NOT installed or scheduled until `leroy enable <feature>`.

boardroom.label: Boardroom
boardroom.plain: A simulated C-suite that debates your decisions and watches the system for improvements.
boardroom.token_cost: token-heavy; recommended only on Claude Max
boardroom.wires: registers the boardroom scene cron + enables the boardroom module

morning_briefing.label: Morning briefing
morning_briefing.plain: A short daily digest each morning; can fold in boardroom findings.

email_digests.label: Email digests / outbound
email_digests.plain: I can send you digests or draft outbound email on a schedule.
email_digests.wires: registers the digest/outbound cron (outbound stays draft-gated by the gmail guard)

scheduled_automations.label: Scheduled automations / crons
scheduled_automations.plain: Timed background jobs: self-heal on a timer, automatic backups, watchdogs, etc.
scheduled_automations.token_cost: varies by job; can add up if several run
scheduled_automations.wires: registers the selected background crons (self-heal timer, backup, watchdog)
